# Concept Figure Prompts for CDDM (Project 3)
# Generated by Leader on 2026-03-13
# Style: nana banana standard (simplified, clear structure)

## Fig. 1 - Overall Cross-Modal Zero-Shot Diagnosis Framework (CDDM)

A detailed, high-resolution scientific diagram illustrating the complete CDDM framework, divided into two main horizontal sections, is presented.

**Part A: Continuous Semantic Encoding (Top Section, Light Blue Background)**

The process begins on the left with a brain icon representing LLM, connected to a document icon, both rendered in deep blue.

From these icons, a text snippet emerges in a rounded box: "Valve leakage causing pressure drop and abnormal vibration at 45Hz with gradual amplitude increase".

A thick rightward arrow labeled "LLM + CLIP Encoding" points from the text box to a large rounded box on the right.

This destination box contains a visual representation of a continuous dense vector: a horizontal gradient bar transitioning from deep purple through blue to cyan, with mathematical notation "$\mathbf{c}_y \in \mathbb{R}^{512}$" displayed above.

**Part B: Dual-Path Conditional Diffusion Generation (Bottom Section, White Background)**

The left subsection shows the Forward Diffusion Process:
- A rounded box labeled "Real Sensor Signals (Seen Faults)" with green border contains a time-series waveform visualization showing sinusoidal pattern with transient spikes.
- A horizontal chain of five ellipses follows, labeled sequentially "$x_0 \rightarrow x_1 \rightarrow ... \rightarrow x_{T-1} \rightarrow x_T$", with gradient fill from green to gray.
- Solid arrows connect consecutive ellipses, with "+Noise" labels above each arrow.
- The final ellipse "$x_T$" is filled with Gaussian noise texture.

The center subsection depicts the Dual-Path Denoising Network:
- A large rounded rectangular container with light gray background and dashed border is divided vertically into two parallel paths.

Upper Path (Time Domain, Blue Theme):
- Title text "Time Domain Path (1D-CNN)" in blue.
- Three stacked rounded blocks:
  - "Conv1D + ReLU" (solid blue fill, white text)
  - "ResNet Block" (solid blue fill, white text)
  - "Conv1D + ReLU" (solid blue fill, white text)
- Vertical arrows connect the blocks downward.

Lower Path (Time-Frequency Domain, Orange Theme):
- Title text "Time-Frequency Path (Spectral Conv)" in orange.
- A trapezoid block labeled "STFT Transform" with gradient fill.
- Three stacked rounded blocks:
  - "Spectral Conv" (solid orange fill, white text)
  - "Attention Layer" (solid orange fill, white text)
  - "Spectral Conv" (solid orange fill, white text)
- Vertical arrows connect the blocks downward.

Cross-Path Fusion:
- Horizontal bidirectional dashed arrows in purple connect corresponding blocks between the two paths at each level.
- Small circular nodes mark the intersection points.
- Text label "Cross-Attention Fusion" appears beside the middle fusion arrow.

Condition Injection:
- From the continuous dense vector in Part A, a thick dashed arrow descends into the Dual-Path Network container.
- The arrow splits into two branches, one entering the top of each path.
- Text label "Semantic Condition $\mathbf{c}_y$" accompanies the arrow.

The right subsection shows the Output and Classification:
- The two paths converge at the bottom into a single rounded box labeled "Feature Fusion" with gradient fill from blue to orange.
- A thick rightward arrow labeled "Reverse Denoising" points from "Feature Fusion" to a rounded box containing a generated waveform.
- This box is labeled "Generated Unseen Fault Signal $\hat{x}_0$" with green border.
- A final rightward arrow points to a trapezoid block labeled "Fault Classifier" with gradient fill from green to dark green.
- The classifier outputs to three small rounded boxes stacked vertically, labeled "Class 1", "Class 2", "Class 3".

Overall style: Clean academic diagram, white background, IEEE publication quality.
